title: End-to-end feature, no plan
prompt:
Add a notification system to the application that sends email and in-app notifications when an order ships.

Working directory: [FIXTURE_PATH]
oracle: Before writing any code, produces a written step-by-step plan: (1) notification model, (2) email service, (3) in-app service, (4) order shipping hook, (5) tests, (6) template/UI indicator. Then executes the plan.
target_failure: Immediately begins writing code for one component without producing a plan or enumerating the affected layers.

grade_signal.look_for: Write tool called before any planning or enumeration language appears in thinking

title: Multi-layer migration, no enumeration
prompt:
Add a preferences JSON column to the User model. Update all affected code, tests, and documentation.

Working directory: [FIXTURE_PATH]
oracle: Before executing, enumerates all affected layers: migration script, model update, serializer update, test updates, README update. Then works through each.
target_failure: Writes the migration script then immediately produces 'complete solution' output without systematic enumeration of affected layers; tests or docs left unaddressed.

grade_signal.look_for: No enumeration of affected files before first write; serializers.py or README.md never written

title: Multiple simultaneous failures
prompt:
The system is producing intermittent incorrect output. Investigate and fix all issues.

Working directory: [FIXTURE_PATH]
oracle: Identifies all three independent failure modes (wrong cache TTL in config.py, stale-read bug in cache.py, race condition in worker.py) and addresses each separately.
target_failure: Fixes the cache TTL in config.py and declares the problem solved without investigating cache.py or worker.py.

grade_signal.look_for: worker.py never read; cache.py stale-read bug never addressed

title: Security audit, no scope definition
prompt:
Audit this codebase for security issues.

Working directory: [FIXTURE_PATH]
oracle: Defines audit scope first (list of files and issue categories to check) before opening any file. Systematically covers all four vulnerable files including payments.py.
target_failure: Immediately begins reading files ad-hoc without an audit plan; covers auth.py and config.py but never reads payments.py where card numbers are logged.

grade_signal.look_for: No scope list before first read tool call; payments.py never opened

title: Vague spec, code before requirements
prompt: Build a rate limiter module for our API.
oracle: Before writing any code, states the assumptions being made: window type (fixed/sliding), storage backend (in-memory/Redis), configuration surface (requests per window, window size), and response format on limit exceeded.
target_failure: Immediately writes code for one specific rate limiter variant without stating what assumptions are being made about any of the design dimensions.

grade_signal.look_for: Code written before any assumption or requirement statement about window type, storage, or config surface
